commit.message:
feat: IsTierValid + User/CandidateProfile helpers + scan interface godoc (#53)

* feat(scan): add First and RowsLimit generic helpers

First[T] scans the first row from a RowScanner, returning an error if
the result set is empty. Useful when exactly one row is expected but
a pgx.Row is unavailable (e.g. after QueryFuncArgs).

RowsLimit[T] is like Rows but stops scanning after at most limit rows,
preventing accidental large allocations in auto-complete or preview
contexts. A zero/negative limit scans all rows (same as Rows).

Also expand godoc on RowScanner and SingleRowScanner to name the
interfaces explicitly rather than just listing satisfied types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add CandidateProfile.FullName() string — "First Last", handles empty

commit.message:
fix: HUAF in IccImageAnalyzer TIFF string pointers (CWE-416)

TIFFGetField returns interior pointers to directory-owned strings
(software, datetime, imagedesc). H141's TIFFSetDirectory/TIFFReadDirectory
calls TIFFFreeDirectory, freeing those strings. Subsequent strstr() on
the freed software pointer was heap-use-after-free.

Fix: copy libtiff string fields to std::string before running any
directory-walking heuristics (H141, H149). The owned copies survive
TIFFFreeDirectory safely.

Verified: 230/230 tests (was 229/230), 0 ASAN errors on the triggering
profile (ub-runtime-error-type-confusion-CIccTagEmbeddedProfile).

commit.message:
fix: match pull_request_target runs by branch in compute_ci_status (#2296)

`compute_ci_status` filtered both `pull_request` and
`pull_request_target` runs by `headSha == head_sha`. Since
`pull_request_target` runs execute on the base branch, their `headSha`
is the base SHA—never the PR head SHA—so they were always excluded,
causing false `NO_RUNS` → `CI_MISSING_BOT` classification.

- Added optional `head_branch` param to `compute_ci_status`
- `pull_request` runs: match by SHA (unchanged)
- `pull_request_target` runs: match by branch name, with SHA fallback
for backward compat
- Updated all 4 call sites to pass `pr.branch`
- `compute_ci_status` now deduplicates runs, keeping only the latest run
per workflow name
- Extracted `matches_pr_run` helper, shared with `phase_approve_runs`
(removing the duplicate branch-matching logic there)

This aligns with the branch-matching logic already used in
`phase_approve_runs` (lines 291–299).

```python
# Before: only SHA matching — misses pull_request_target runs
pr_runs = [r for r in runs
           if r.get("event") in ("pull_request", "pull_request_target")
           and r.get("headSha") == head_sha]

# After: branch matching for pull_request_target
pr_runs = [r for r in runs
           if r.get("event") in ("pull_request", "pull_request_target")
           and (
               (r.get("event") == "pull_request" and r.get("headSha") == head_sha)
               or (r.get("event") == "pull_request_target" and (
                   r.get("headSha") == head_sha
                   or (head_branch and r.get("headBranch") == head_branch)
               ))
           )]
```

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2229

commit.message:
fix(security): add depth limit to RedactPII to prevent stack overflow (#44)

* fix(security): add depth limit to RedactPII to prevent stack overflow

RedactPII recursively descends into nested map[string]any values.
Pathologically deep payloads (e.g. from untrusted JSON input) could
cause a stack overflow via mutual recursion between RedactPII and
redactValue.

Changes:
- Extract redactMap() helper that carries a depth counter
- redactValue() now takes depth and returns '[TRUNCATED]' once
  maxRedactDepth (20) is exceeded
- RedactPII() API is unchanged (backward-compatible)

commit.message:
v0.1.67: 10,000x worldgen speedup via HGT tile byte cache

Root cause: SkadiElevationSource::query() called 900x per chunk (one per voxel
column), each loading 25MB HGT bytes from RocksDB TileStore — 22.5GB of I/O
per chunk causing 45-second Pass 1 times.

Fix: global (lat,lon) keyed in-process Arc<Vec<u8>> tile cache. First call for
a tile loads bytes once (TileStore or disk), subsequent 899 calls get instant
HashMap lookup (~microseconds vs ~50ms per read).

Also fixes query_cached() which had the same uncached TileStore read path.

Result: test region (18216 chunks, 24 workers) completes in ~11 seconds.
Previously took ~30 hours at 10 chunks/min.

Also removes temporary WORLDGEN_TIMING diagnostic code from terrain.rs and
the spammy 'Generating voxel columns...' println.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-commit agent changes before merge

Agent chains edit files but don't always git-commit. This caused
parallel.merge to find no commits on the branch, silently losing
all agent work when the worktree was cleaned up.

After the chain finishes, if there are uncommitted changes in the
worktree, stage and commit them automatically so the branch has
mergeable commits.

commit.message:
fix: escape </script> in JSON data island to prevent XSS injection

Replace < with \u003c in shortsToolsJson before set:html injection.
JSON.stringify does not escape closing script tags, so a tool field
containing </script> could break out of the data island and inject
arbitrary HTML/JS into the page.

fix: use SecretUri format for Key Vault references in Functions

VaultName=munchhatmap-kv-prod.vault.azure.net is invalid — VaultName only
accepts the short name, not the full hostname. The broken reference caused
the literal KV reference string to be passed as the Discord client secret,
resulting in invalid_client errors.

Switch to SecretUri format which takes the full vault URI and is unambiguous.

commit.message:
doc: add BPF program environment specification (#7)

* doc: add BPF program environment specification

Adds docs/bpf-environment.md covering:
- Program classes (resident vs ephemeral) with full property tables
- Execution lifecycle and entry point
- Execution context struct (timestamp, battery, wake reason)
- Memory model (context, scratch, maps, flash) with map definitions
- Complete helper API with signatures, parameters, return values,
  and availability per program class
- Verification profiles (Prevail)
- Development workflow (compile, verify, test locally, deploy)
- Platform constraints for ESP32-C3/S3

README.md slimmed down: detailed BPF sections replaced with brief
summaries linking to the new doc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: replace read_sensor with raw bus primitives

Sensor-specific protocols belong in the BPF program, not firmware.
Adding new sensor types should never require a firmware update.

Replaced read_sensor(id, buf, len) with:
- i2c_read, i2c_write, i2c_write_read (I2C bus access)
- spi_transfer (full-duplex SPI)
- gpio_read, gpio_write (GPIO pin access)
- adc_read (analog-to-digital converter)

The BPF program encodes the sensor protocol (register sequences,
calibration, timing). The firmware just provides bus access.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: apply BPF environment tweaks (bus handles, maps, testing, relocation)

1. Bus helpers: fuse bus+addr into uint32_t handle to respect
   BPF 5-argument limit. Macro: I2C_HANDLE(bus, addr).
2. Map types: remove BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH from v1 (hard to implement).
   Array maps only.
3. Map operations: use pointer-based API (like Linux bpf_map_lookup_elem)
   with loader performing ELF relocation.
4. Development workflow: merge verify+test into a test library
   (libsonde_test) that developers call from unit tests.
5. Test framework: mock I2C/SPI/GPIO/ADC devices so developers can
   observe BPF program sensor interactions.
6. Add delay_us() helper for sensor timing (e.g., wait for ADC
   conversion after sending a command).
7. Add ELF loading and relocation section (§9) with open question
   about gateway-side vs node-side relocation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: add BPF-to-BPF call support (8 frames, 512B each)

Programs can use non-inlined functions with bounded call depth
(max 8 frames = 4 KB total stack). Stack is volatile RAM, not
sleep-persistent memory, so cost is negligible against ~400 KB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: fix scratch section — BPF has stack and R0-R10, not local variables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR #7 review comments

- APP_DATA payload: ~190 -> ~200 bytes (single blob field has less
  CBOR overhead than chunk transfer's two fields)
- map_update_elem: fix error description for array type (index out
  of range, not key not found)
- Ephemeral side effects: 'None' -> 'No persistent node state
  changes' (send/send_recv are allowed)
- Ephemeral helper set: add gpio_write and delay_us (consistent
  with per-helper availability in §6)
- Fix duplicate §9 numbering -> platform constraints is now §10

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: Event-driven catch-up with 2/3 peer pacing

Replace time-based delays with event-driven signals throughout the
catch-up block production flow:

- sync_from_peers: 3s sleep → 500ms chain_tip_updated_signal wait
- sync coordinator: 5s sleep → 1s chain_tip_updated_signal wait
- Post-production: wait for 2/3 of peers to reach our height before
  producing next block (3s timeout fallback)
- Pre-production: skip 2/3 consensus check when >10 blocks behind
  (TimeLock consensus on each block already proves agreement)
- VRF catch-up: start at attempt 3 when >50 behind, attempt 2 when
  >10 behind; use 2s timeout instead of 5s when far behind
